records.evidence: README.md:11-14 — operational gotcha ("One thing worth knowing", "silently binds a random port") addressed to anyone running the dev server locally after a crash, sitting in the user-facing README's Usage section; CLAUDE.md already has the Gotchas section (CLAUDE.md:3) where this class of tribal knowledge lives. Note: line 11 also opens with the usage sentence 'Run `exporter run --tenant NAME`.', which is not part of the extracted passage and must remain

proposal.text: - **Delete `.portlock` before restarting the dev server after any crash** — a stale port lock file makes the next start silently bind a random port.

records.evidence: README.md:16-18 — a caveat ("Note that ... which only matters if you diff exports across platforms") about export newline behavior sitting in the README's Usage section; the export-behavior claim doc docs/reference/export.md:4-5 already carries the binary-mode claim (`src/export.py:4`, whose comment reads 'opens in binary mode to control newlines explicitly') and is where this consequence belongs

proposal.text: - On Windows builds the exporter drops the trailing newline (binary-mode writer, `src/export.py:4`) — exports diffed across platforms differ by that byte.
